title: Coh Veshiv
content:

{{Quote|A man of my rank deserves a cruiser to properly convey my importance. After all, that black-hearted lackey has a Star Destroyer under his personal whim. While I see no need to divert a Star Destroyer, a cruiser really isn't so much to ask, is it…|Imperial Advisor Coh Veshiv complains about his mode of transportation, in comparison to that of the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="Orbit" />}}
'''Coh Veshiv''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] who oversaw the [[Bormea sector/Legends|Bormea]] and [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sectors]], as well as the [[Ringali Shell]], by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. A former [[Senator/Legends|senator]], Veshiv was a master at discovering hidden secrets and blackmailing people, and used his talent to rise through the ranks of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] until he was part of its upper echelons, surpassed only by those closest to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. In 0 BBY, Veshiv was sent from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] on a tour of the [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] he oversaw on the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Empire Forever]]'' after he irritated Palpatine. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], around three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the start of his tour, Veshiv was [[Capture of Coh Veshiv|kidnapped]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]–allied [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] crew of the former Imperial [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]''. Prior to Veshiv's departure from the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]], the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' convinced him to board the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] under the false pretense of being an Imperial [[Starship/Legends|vessel]]. Veshiv was then handed over to the Alliance.

==Biography==
{{Quote|In other news, Imperial Center Security has announced the sudden and tragic death of Imperial Advisor Coh Veshiv in a space accident.|Imperial HoloVison reporter [[Ashii Nermani]] contributes to the covering up of the capture of Veshiv|<ref name="Orbit" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Coh Veshiv was heavily involved in the [[Politics/Legends|politics]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Orbit" /> Sometime prior to the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate's]] [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolution]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Hope">{{Film|IV}}</ref> Veshiv served as a [[Senator/Legends|senator]]. However, Veshiv built his career beyond the rank of senator through an art at which he was a master: blackmailing. Veshiv used the secrets of other Imperial politicians that he had discovered and traded favors for his silence. Using his skills at that art, he soon became a powerful member of the [[Imperial Court/Legends|Imperial Court]] on the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and rose to become an [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]] to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="Orbit" />

As an Imperial [[Advisor/Legends|Advisor]], Veshiv was responsible for the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' [[Bormea sector/Legends|Bormea]] and [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sectors]] and represented the [[Ringali Shell]], an area that passed through both [[Sector/Legends|sectors]], in the Imperial Court. The Advisor was also kept busy keeping the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] and [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moffs]] up to date with the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] wishes. Veshiv reported certain secrets to Palpatine, who found the Imperial Advisor subtle and amusing. However, Veshiv inadvertently irritated Palpatine—during a search to find dirty secrets about one of the Empire's [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]], the Advisor discovered illegal transfers of money. After the Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] was not intimidated by Veshiv's blackmailing, the Advisor told Palpatine of the transfers. However, Palpatine was the one who had requested that the Grand Admiral use those diverted funds for top secret research on [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Annoyed that Veshiv had damaged the covert operation's security, Palpatine had high-ranking Imperial Advisor [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] move Veshiv away from Coruscant.<ref name="Orbit" />

Veshiv was sent from Coruscant and started an inspection of the areas he oversaw. For the inspection, Veshiv used the ''[[Empire Forever]]'', a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] under the helm of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Annance]] which had much of its interior room changed to recreational space for Veshiv. The ''Empire Forever'' also had an [[arboretum]] that housed [[Plant/Legends|plant]] life native to the Advisor's [[Coh Veshiv's homeworld|homeworld]]. Though it had been modified for speed and durability, the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was ordered to avoid combat in order to protect Veshiv.<ref name="Orbit" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{FarOrbitDate}}</ref> Veshiv and the ''Empire Forever'' arrived at the planet [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]], almost three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the start of the inspection tour. By then, Veshiv, who was unsatisfied with the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]], had made multiple requests for a larger ship. He had also managed to annoy Annance, who was a patient man.<ref name="Orbit" />

{{RPGseed}}
[[File:Coh_Veshiv_capture.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Coh Veshiv is captured by the privateers.]]
After three [[Standard day/Legends|days]]' stay at Esseles, Veshiv and the ''Empire Forever'' prepared to leave from jump point 180:45:115, near a [[Golan III Space Defense NovaGun]], [[Platform 8127/121]]. However, prior to Veshiv's departure, the Imperial [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'' arrived near Esseles, and Captain [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]] of the ''Far Orbit'' hailed the ''Empire Forever'' and offered to ferry Veshiv around for the next part of his journey, sending the orders that he had received to do so to Annance. After Veshiv was informed of the transfer, he and his two [[Aide/Legends|aides]], [[Vestig]] and [[Pich]], along with his group of veteran [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], were transported to the ''Far Orbit'' by Veshiv's personal [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] ''[[Myrenia]]''.<ref name="Orbit" />

As soon as Veshiv arrived on the ship, he started complaining that he wanted a [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], not a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]], and even noted that the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]'s [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] - whom he referred to as a "black-hearted lackey" - had the [[Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Executor]]'' at his disposal. Once the EF76 entered [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] for Veshiv's next destination, the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' turned on Veshiv and his escort. Unbeknownst to Veshiv and the others, the crew of the frigate had become [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]—which was currently engaged with the Empire in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]—after [[Mutiny on the Far Orbit|mutinying]] against the Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the ''Far Orbit''. After intercepting an Imperial transmission that pinpointed the location of Veshiv, the ''Far Orbit'' and its crew had decided to try to capture the Advisor. As the crew attempted to [[Capture of Coh Veshiv|take]] Veshiv, the stormtrooper guards fought back, attempting to reach the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] to send for help. This caused a [[Lightfight|firefight]] to erupt in the corridors of the vessel. In the end, the crew overwhelmed Veshiv's guards, and they captured the Imperial Advisor.<ref name="Orbit" />
{{Endgame}}

Veshiv was sent to the [[Prison/Legends|brig]], and he told the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' many Imperial political secrets that were useless to them in exchange for his continued health. Veshiv was eventually handed over to the Alliance for a ransom of 10,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. After the loss of Veshiv to privateers, the Imperials dedicated resources to finding and eliminating the threat of the ''Far Orbit''.<ref name="Orbit" /> Palpatine was angry at Grand Admiral [[Osvald Teshik]], who commanded the [[Imperial Center Oversector]] which contained Esseles, for letting Rebel-allied privateers take Veshiv, and he sent Teshik on a [[Battle of Andalia|suicide mission]] to the [[Andalia system/Legends|Andalia system]].<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> To cover up the disappearance of Veshiv, the news net [[Imperial HoloVision]] reported that Veshiv had [[Death/Legends|died]] after his [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] hit a stray [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] while he was on the inspection tour. Imperial HoloVision also stated that the Empire had yet to find a replacement for Veshiv.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|She was reported in the Esseles system two days ago. She has captured Imperial Advisor Coh Veshiv, a valued and loyal servant of our master.|Imperial Advisor Sate Pestage informs Captain [[Vocis Kenit]] that the ''Far Orbit'' has captured Veshiv|<ref name="Orbit" />}}
[[File:Rollingtheeyes.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Veshiv managed to annoy many a person, including Annance.]]
Coh Veshiv was vain, and both gluttonous and consumptive. The Advisor was in a neglected physical state, with an unhealthy pallor and overall poor vitality, while his weight actually hindered his movements. Possessing a sharp mind and a perfect memory and recollection, he was knowledgeable in the dealings of the Imperial Court, as well as how bureaucracy worked in general. Obsessive about the secrets of others, he had a particular talent in discovering them, as well as improprieties or embarrassing facts about other Imperial political members. Veshiv was also a master at blackmailing, and he used that and the secrets he discovered to rise in political rank. He would usually use a lesser secret to start the extortion, before eventually revealing how he knew more important things. In exchange for his silence on certain secrets he knew, Veshiv would receive favors from those he blackmailed.<ref name="Orbit" />

After being sent away from Coruscant on the ''Empire Forever'', Veshiv was annoyed that he was on a corvette, which was not any better than the ships that had transported him when he was a senator. The lack of an important vessel to transport him sat uneasily with him; Pestage had actually arranged it as an insult. Upon finally receiving a better ship than a corvette, Veshiv immediately complained that it was a frigate and not a cruiser. Self-centered, Veshiv was considered annoying by most people, including Annance and the crew of the ''Far Orbit''. A coward, Veshiv sweated as the battle was fought on the ''Far Orbit'', even though he carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]] and was able to use it. Besides the [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], he also carried around a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. He was somewhat skilled in the use of [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcrafts]], and had some technical abilities that included the programming of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and [[Computer/Legends|computers]]. Veshiv was also fairly knowledgable about various topics, ranging from alien [[Species/Legends|species]] to business, and from [[Language/Legends|languages]] to culture. Additionally, he knew a bit about law enforcement and scholarly material.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
Coh Veshiv was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'' by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]], and appeared in the [[roleplaying game]] adventure ''[[The Capture of Coh Veshiv]]'' from the book. ''The Far Orbit Project'' was a supplement to [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Orbit" />

Although the mission in ''The Far Orbit Project'' suggests that players lure Coh Veshiv onto the ''Far Orbit'', players can capture him any way they want. The book outlines two different ways in which the battle over Veshiv can be fought, depending on when the crew tries to capture him after he is on the ''Far Orbit''. If the players attempt to seize Veshiv prior to when they enter hyperspace, it is possible for the ''Myrenia'' and her [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] to warn the Golan III [[Space station/Legends|space station]] and the ''Empire Forever'' of the ''Far Orbit''{{'s}} true colors. If a warning is released, the Golan III and the CR90 attempt to stop the frigate. However, if the crew jumps on Veshiv after they enter hyperspace, the shuttle cannot warn them. This article assumes that they enter hyperspace before trying to seize Veshiv.<ref name="Orbit" />
